label: PASS
rationale: Revert on the Admin SP object is mandatory, operates in a read-write Admin SP session, and has an empty result list; this invocation meets those requirements.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Revert signature returns an empty list, but the target returns a non-empty result value.

label: FAIL
rationale: Revert operates within a read-write Admin SP session; the active session was opened with Write=0, so successful Revert is not compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: After successful Revert on the Admin SP object outside a transaction, the TPer must abort the session immediately after status, so a subsequent Set succeeding in that same session is noncompliant.

label: PASS
rationale: SID was previously successfully authenticated and the feature descriptor behavior is 0x00, so Admin SP Revert sets C_PIN_SID to C_PIN_MSID; a new SID session using MSID may succeed.

label: FAIL
rationale: After SID-authenticated Admin SP Revert with behavior 0x00, C_PIN_SID must be set to C_PIN_MSID, so authentication with the prior changed PIN should not succeed.

label: FAIL
rationale: Because the Locking SP was activated and not Manufactured-Inactive at Admin SP Revert, successful Revert must remove user data and eradicate media encryption keys; reading the old written pattern afterward is noncompliant.
